items.correct: Holding minimal stock and receiving inputs only when needed for production — reducing storage costs but increasing vulnerability to supply disruption.
items.misconception: Just-in-time means delivering products to customers exactly on time.
items.random_wrong: Just-in-time means finishing work right before the deadline.
items.why: JIT is about INPUT stock to production, not output delivery to customers. Students misread the term as a delivery promise; it is an internal stock control philosophy.

items.q: What is a key disadvantage of being a sole trader?
items.correct: Unlimited liability — the owner's personal assets are at risk if the business fails, because there is no legal separation between owner and business.
items.misconception: The main disadvantage is that sole traders can't sell any products.
items.random_wrong: Sole traders must employ at least one other person by law.
items.why: Sole traders can sell products and don't need employees. The key legal disadvantage is unlimited liability — the personal risk exposure, which is why many businesses become limited companies as they grow.

items.correct: Cost advantages a firm gains as it grows — average cost per unit falls because fixed costs spread over more units, bulk discounts, and specialisation become possible.
items.misconception: Economies of scale means total costs decrease as the business grows.
items.random_wrong: Economies of scale means a business saves the economy money.
items.why: Total costs RISE with growth — it's AVERAGE cost per unit that falls. Students confuse total and average, missing the precise meaning of the concept.

items.q: Ryanair's strategy is best described as:
items.why: Correct answer: Cost leadership. “Differentiation” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Kim & Mauborgne. “Porter” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many generic strategies did Porter identify?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
